items.question: In 5-fold cross-validation, each sample appears in the validation fold how many times?
items.explanation: Each fold serves as validation exactly once across the k rounds.

items.question: Stratified k-fold is primarily used to:
options: Preserve class proportions in each fold
Reduce training time by skipping folds
Replace the need for any test set
items.answer: Preserve class proportions in each fold
items.explanation: Stratification keeps rare classes present in every validation slice.

items.question: The usual CV score reported for model comparison is:
options: The mean of per-fold validation metrics
The median of hyperparameter grid scores from inner tuning
items.answer: The mean of per-fold validation metrics
items.explanation: Mean fold score estimates expected validation performance; std shows stability.

items.question: Fitting StandardScaler on all of X before cross_val_score causes:
options: Data leakage that can inflate CV scores
items.answer: Data leakage that can inflate CV scores
items.explanation: Validation folds contribute to global scale statistics used during training.

items.question: Nested cross-validation separates:
options: Inner tuning from outer performance estimation
Train and test sets into equal halves only
items.answer: Inner tuning from outer performance estimation
items.explanation: Inner loop selects settings; outer loop scores on held-out data not used for that selection.
